channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: House lawmakers hold hearing to further investigate Florida insurance companies
item.description: House lawmakers gathered at the statehouse Friday for a hearing to look into a bombshell report that Florida insurance companies were "ripping off" Floridians and misleading the legislature, claiming to be broke.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate unanimously approves fix to prevent $1B cuts for District of Columbia, sending bill to House
item.description: The Senate has swiftly approved legislation that would allow the District of Columbia to keep its budget intact.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rubio says South Africa’s ambassador to the US 'is no longer welcome' in the country
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio says South Africa’s ambassador to the United States “is no longer welcome” in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democrats set to host town halls targeting vulnerable House Republicans
item.description: Democratic committees are set to host town halls across the country to target vulnerable House Republicans ahead of the 2026 midterms.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Proposals to cut down on bad driving cross political lanes in Minnesota session
item.description: A variety of bills that aim to make Minnesota’s roads less treacherous are rolling their way through the legislative process. It could wind up as one area of bipartisan consensus in a session where that is a paramount.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: DOGE lease cancellations: These government offices could be closed in Minnesota
item.description: The General Services Administration, working with Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, has notified landlords that federal agencies will be terminating hundreds of leases for offices and buildings in the coming months — including a dozen in Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Burn pit fund for veterans on chopping block in GOP spending bill
item.description: The fund helps former service members sickened by military toxic exposures, including burn pit smoke and Agent Orange contamination.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Senate passes 6-month funding bill, sending measure to Trump’s desk
item.description: The Senate passed a six-month spending bill on Friday, hours before a government shutdown, overcoming sharp Democratic opposition to the measure and sending it to President Donald Trump to be signed into law.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Two judges in mass firings cases order Trump administration to rehire probationary workers for now
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Two federal judges handed down orders requiring President Donald Trump's administration to rehire thousands, if not tens of thousands, of probationary workers let go in mass firings across multiple agencies Thursday, slowing down for now the president's dramatic downsizing of th
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, BRIAN WITTE and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin will always be a threat to stability in Europe: Dissident
item.description: Ukrainian leaders have already agreed to a 30-day halt in fighting, and now, U.S. diplomats are seeking Russia’s acceptance.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about Mark Carney, elected next Canadian prime minister
item.description: Mark Carney, a former central banker, won his Liberal Party's election in a landslide on Sunday to replace Justin Trudeau as prime minister of Canada. He will be sworn into office in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams’ approval rating reaches record low ahead of election
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – The mayor’s race is heating up just as Mayor Eric Adams’ approval rating hits a new low, according to a new poll from Quinnipiac University. A new poll shows only 20% of voters approve of how Adams is handling his post, while 67% disapprove.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11
